diagnostic: The founder builds the rocket. The CEO flies it. Most founders never make the transition because they cannot offload the cognitive load of building while steering. The boardroom carries the building so you can steer.
answer: The founder builds the rocket. The CEO flies it. Most founders never make the transition because they cannot offload the cognitive load of building while steering. The boardroom carries the building so you can steer.
description: Human bandwidth ceiling — the leader moves from finance to marketing to production in a single afternoon, crushed by context switches.
symptom: 15–25 hours per week in alignment meetings. Decision fatigue. Founder burnout. Family strain.
